results.title: Portfolio Risk Modeling, Model Governance - Associate | Jobflarely
highlights:
Portfolio Risk Modeling, Model Governance - Associate | Jobflarely

Jobflarely.← All Jobs

# Portfolio Risk Modeling, Model Governance - Associate

Posted May 25, 2026

BlackRock is one of the world’s preeminent asset management firms, and they are seeking a hands-on Quantitative Associate to join the Portfolio Risk team within Aladdin Financial Engineering. This role focuses on quantitative research, model development, testing, and implementation, contributing to the team’s AI transformation journey.

### Responsibilities

- Research, design, and back-test portfolio risk models using Python-based infrastructure
- Work hands-on with large and complex financial datasets, ensuring data quality and robustness of results
- Collaborate closely with software engineers to test, productionize, and maintain models
- Support existing models in production, including investigation and resolution of model-related questions from internal stakeholders and clients
- Develop and enhance testing, validation, back-testing, and quality-control frameworks
- Contribute to the team’s AI transformation journey, with a focus on applying AI, ML, and automation to model governance processes, such as: Model validation and back-testing
- Testing and quality control
- Documentation and reproducibility checks
- Research-to-production code migration
- Clearly document and communicate model assumptions, results, and limitations to both technical and non-technical audiences

Skills

- Master's degree (e.g., MFE) or PhD in a quantitative field such as Finance, Economics, Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, or Engineering
- Strong hands-on programming experience, primarily in Python (R a plus)
- Experience working with large datasets and applying statistical, econometric, or quantitative techniques
- Solid understanding of financial markets, financial products, and basic economics
- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with high attention to detail
- Clear written and verbal communication skills in English
- Ability to work effectively in a collaborative, team-oriented environment
- Critical thinking and intellectual curiosity
- Strong ownership of work and accountability for quality
- Ability to translate complex quantitative ideas into practical, usable solutions
- Comfort working across disciplines (quant research, engineering, risk, product)
- Interest in building robust, scalable, and well-governed analytical systems
- Innovative thinking balanced with sound judgment and practicality
- Exposure to machine learning and AI techniques, particularly as applied to financial or time-series data
- Experience applying AI, ML, or automation to model lifecycle and governance workflows, such as validation, back-testing, testing, monitoring, documentation, or code migration
- Knowledge of fixed income and/or equity risk factor models
- Understanding of portfolio theory and risk analytics
- Experience designing rigorous testing and back-testing frameworks
- Familiarity with building scalable and repeatable research or modeling processes
- Strong software engineering practices (clean, well-tested code)
- Experience with Unix/Linux and Git

### Benefits

- Healthcare
- Leave benefits
- Retirement benefits
- Strong retirement plan
- Tuition reimbursement
- Support for working parents
- Flexible Time Off (FTO)

Company Overview

- BlackRock is an investment company that offers its services to institutions, intermediaries, foundations, and individual investors. It was founded in 1988, and is headquartered in New York, New York, USA, with a workforce of 10001+ employees. Its website is http://www.blackrock.com. Company H1B Sponsorship

- BlackRock has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 1 in 2026, 18 in 2025, 34 in 2024, 7 in 2023, 37 in 2022, 31 in 2021, 27 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

# Associate, Quantitative Research job at BlackRock in Sausalito, CA
[...]
Title: Associate, Quantitative Research
[...]
### Location: San Francisco, CA Sausalito, CA
[...]
We are seeking a highly quantitative and motivated Associate to join the Quantitative Investment Solutions (QIS) team, a specialized unit within Aperio Portfolio Management, operating under BlackRock's SMA Solutions business. QIS is responsible for driving strategic product innovation, developing tax-managed investment strategies, and advancing quantitative research. The team spearheads the creation of cutting-edge tax-advantaged solutions, encompasses direct indexing, active long/short, option overlays, factor tilts, concentration management and more. QIS also plays a pivotal role in client engagement and internal business development collaboration, supporting product activation and commercial growth.
[...]
The Associate will contribute to all facets of the quantitative strategy research process, including building research infrastructure, managing back-tests and simulations, and generating quant analytics to support the design and development of tax-managed investment strategies. The ideal candidate will demonstrate the ability to solve complex challenges related to tax optimization, systematic active portfolio construction, and risk modeling for long/short equity and option-based strategies. The role requires advanced quantitative and programming skills, consistent detail-oriented problem-solving capabilities, effective communication in verbal and written form, and the ability to work under a collaborative environment. Success in this position also depends on the ability to manage and prioritize complicated sets of tasks, take initiative and display responsibility for quality and timeliness of output. The title and compensation will be commensurate with experience.
[...]
- Assist in the design, advancement, implementation, and maintenance of research infrastructure and tools to support quantitative research, investment strategy development and evaluation.

[...]

results.title: Vice President, Alternative Data Strategy and Execution - ImpactAlpha
highlights:
# Vice President, Alternative Data Strategy and Execution
[...]
- EDGE
- Company BlackRock
- Role
- Location Boston, MA
[...]
### About BlackRock
[...]
BlackRock, founded in 1988, is a global investment management corporation based in New York City. As of 2023, it is the world’s largest asset manager, with over $9 trillion in assets under management. Known for its commitment to financial integrity and excellence, BlackRock provides a range of investment and risk management services to institutional and retail clients worldwide. BlackRock’s mission is to foster an inclusive culture and support employee career growth. The company embraces technology and data to pioneer next-generation investment strategies. Learn more about BlackRock’s innovative work in sustainability, financial technology, and client solutions by visiting their website or following them on Twitter.
[...]
### About this Role
[...]
As part of BlackRock’s Portfolio Management Group (PMG), you will work on the Alternative Data Strategy and Execution team. This role is crucial in sourcing and prioritizing alternative datasets that enhance investment research and drive alpha. You’ll be at the intersection of investment research, data strategy, and commercial execution, ensuring data opportunities align with BlackRock’s strategic priorities. This position requires an adept communicator and coordinator to partner with cross-functional teams in data evaluation and acquisition. Although not primarily technical, a strong data fluency is essential to partner with technical teams effectively.
[...]
### Responsibilities
[...]
- Partner with investors to identify, prioritize, and source alternative datasets aligned to Alpha Priorities and investment strategies
[...]
- Maintain a strong view of the alternative data landscape, delivering thematic insights, and identifying datasets that enhance existing signals or unlock new opportunities
[...]
- Prioritize competing demands and drive datasets from idea through evaluation to onboarding, balancing speed, impact, and scalability
[...]
- Facilitate dataset evaluation with investors and research teams, ensuring practical usability and investment impact
[...]
- Act as the central point of contact for third-party data inventory, driving visibility, reuse, and discoverability of datasets across teams
[...]
- Engage with a broad range of vendors, supporting strategic data partnerships, negotiations, and commercial assessment of value and fit
[...]
- Coordinate dataset reviews with IDS and control functions, ensuring compliant use of data, including licensing and AI/ML usage rights
[...]
- Partner with Business Managers and platform teams to streamline data acquisition lifecycle and ensure consistent workflows across PMG
[...]
- Connect investors, data teams, engineering, and vendors to coordinate workflows and bring in the right expertise to drive outcomes
[...]
- Support governance initiatives and contribute to improving transparency of data usage and spend across PMG
[...]
- Experience in financial services, data strategy, consulting, or investment research
- Exposure to alternative data and/or investment research workflows
- Familiarity with data licensing, governance, and AI/ML usage considerations
- Experience working across cross-functional teams spanning investment, data, and technology
[...]
- Strong organizational skills
[...]
- Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate between investor needs, data capabilities, and commercial considerations
[...]
- Interest in financial markets and the drivers of performance, from macro events to company-level fundamentals
[...]
- Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience
- 5+ Years relevant experience in financial services, data, or consulting environments
[...]

results.title: AI Engineer – Career Development Office | MIT Sloan School of Management
highlights:
# AI Engineer – Career Development Office | MIT Sloan School of Management
[...]
Whale Rock Capital Management
[...]
# AI Engineer
[...]
on the Technology, Media and Telecom (TMT
[...]
THE ROLEThe AI Engineer will join Whale Rock’s Technology team, reporting to Will Feener, CTO. The primary mandate is practical: take the firm’s AI capabilities to the next level by building, configuring and validating tools that the investment and operations teams rely on every day. This is Whale Rock’s first dedicated AI developer. This is not a role where you’ll be handed a backlog of tickets and a well-documented codebase. The role calls for someone who is energized by that, not deterred by it. The AI Engineer will work across the IT, investment and operations teams to scope and build AI-powered tools, data pipelines, and internal applications that make our research process faster and smarter.
[...]
- Solution Identification
[...]
: Identify high
[...]
impact opportunities across investment, operations and data workflows. Independently scope, prioritize, and drive AI-powered solutions from concept through deployment and iteration.
[...]
- Workflow Engineering & Automation: Design and implement durable, AI-driven workflows including constructing multi-step prompt chains, configuring custom LLM assistants, and automating manual processes that slow down the investment and operations teams.
[...]
- Internal AI Tooling: Develop and maintain the user-facing AI interfaces that the investment and operations teams use daily – whether that’s a custom assistant, a configured enterprise AI platform, or a lightweight internal application built to fit a specific workflow.
[...]
- AI Operations & Technical Support: Serve as the firm’s go-to resource when AI tools break down or underperform. When a workflow hallucinates or fails mid-process, the AI Engineer will investigate the root cause – examining context windows, adjusting model parameters and correcting underlying prompt logic.
[...]
- Model & Tool Evaluation: Stay current on emerging AI models and tools as the market evolves. Assess new options on a structured, ongoing basis – standing up test environments, scoring outputsagainst defined prompt sets, evaluating data-handling and security constraints, and delivering clear, evidence-based recommendations to the CTO on what warrants broader rollout.
[...]
- Data Engineering: Design and maintain data pipelines that pull from financial data vendors, portfolio systems, and research platforms. Normalize, clean, and serve data reliably to internal tools and dashboards. Ensure data is accessible and actionable.
[...]
- Technical Fluency & Hands-On Building: Naturally curious about what AI can do and how to make it work in practice. Keeps up with the latest models and tools, has hands-on experience constructing prompt chains, agentic workflows, and RAG pipelines, and is always looking for the next thing worth testing.
[...]
- Scripting & Prototyping: Proficient in Python or equivalent. Not expected to write production-grade software, but able to build scripts that call APIs, process data, and produce working lightweight prototypes. When the AI tooling fails, this person can still identify and work through problems in code.
[...]
- Strong Product & Technical Judgment: Sound judgement about where AI creates real value and where it doesn’t. Able to distinguish between hype and real utility, prioritize high-impact opportunities, and make sound decisions when evaluating tools, models, and workflows in ambiguous environments.
[...]
- Experience: 1 to 4 years of experience in a solutions engineering, technical product management, forward-deployed engineering, or advanced IT operations role, preferably in a fast-paced tech environment. You understand financial markets. You know what a hedge fund does, you’ve probably followed stocks or built models, and the idea of building tools for investors genuinely excites you.

results.title: Index Fixed Income Algo Engineer, Associate | Hirevector
highlights:
Index Fixed Income Algo Engineer, Associate
[...]
About this role BlackRock is one of the world’s preeminent asset management firms and a premier provider of global investment management, risk management and advisory services to institutional, intermediary, and individual investors around the world. BlackRock offers a range of solutions — from meticulous fundamental and quantitative active management approaches aimed at maximizing outperformance to highly efficient indexing strategies designed to gain broad exposure to the world’s capital markets. Our clients can access our investment solutions through a variety of product structures, including individual and institutional separate accounts, mutual funds and other pooled investment vehicles, and the industry-leading iShares® ETFs. Business Unit Overview BlackRock Global Markets and Index Investments (BGM) brings together BlackRock’s Index Investments, Cash Management, Global Trading, ETF Markets, Securities Lending, and Financing & Financial Resource Management businesses. Together, we provide clients with superior market access and index investment outcomes through: World class talent, operating at the intersection of investment insight, markets expertise, risk management and process design Technology at the heart of our processes, with in-business tech teams that create cutting-edge IP, providing an advantage in quality, scale, and risk control A focus on transforming markets, driving and advocating for more accessible, resilient, and transparent markets to deliver more choice and lower costs for investors Job Purpose/Background: The Index Fixed Income (IFI) team has responsibility for managing Blackrock’s Index Fixed Income portfolios, including Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs), mutual funds, & segregated mandates. The IFI team liaises closely with many areas of Fixed Income and is responsible for ensuring that Fixed Income portfolios are managed efficiently and in a consistent and risk-controlled manner. BlackRock’s IFI business is the leading index investment group in the world. We help investors build better portfolios with the industry’s leading suite of index investment tools. We manage over $1.5 trillion in global index fixed income assets across Developed and Emerging Markets. Our clients include corporate and public pension plans, central banks, sovereign wealth funds and retail investors. The PM Architecture team are designing and developing portfolio construction algorithms to significantly increase automation and productivity across IFI globally. They are also partnering with Technology teams to develop the core platform with which to deploy algorithms robustly at scale. Algorithms have already been successfully deployed which are event-based and automatically propose order lists that rebalance funds back to target. A key focus is developing algorithms and analytics to support the rapid growth of Fixed Income ETFs. A successful candidate will be familiar with management of a wide range of Fixed Income investment styles and have strong technology skills, allowing them to develop both prototypes which make immediate improvements to the PM process and then refine them into production solutions that scale. They will also have the communication skills and enthusiasm to partner with PM, Trading, and Technology teams to guide the integration of these algorithms into the investment process and leverage core BlackRock technology.
[...]
- Design efficient algorithms and optimizers to automate key portfolio construction processes
[...]
- Develop both prototypes and production tools
[...]
- Partner with BlackRock Technology teams to design and develop the core “Algo Platform”
[...]
- Strong quantitative background (at least BA/BS in Math/Science/Engineering/related discipline).
[...]
- Strong programming skills in at least two of the following: Python, Visual Basic, R, MATLAB, Java, C++
[...]
- Working knowledge

# Quantitative Technology & Implementation, Associate

Posted May 28, 2026

BlackRock is focused on transforming wealth management through technology, aiming to enhance client value propositions. The role involves leading client servicing and project management for implementations, understanding business problems, and delivering tailored solutions while collaborating with product managers and engineers.

### Responsibilities

- Help lead the day-to-day client servicing and project management of client implementations and ongoing client engagements
- Help lead client implementations, working to understand their business problems, then design and deliver solutions
- Contribute to the build-out of product capabilities, functioning as the internal representative for clients by driving alignment between the Aladdin Wealth offering and client needs
- Work with product managers, engineers, and researchers to help craft the product offering and support the development of core Aladdin models and risk systems
- Build and deliver technical analyses to help clients better understand Aladdin models and how our offering can be applied their business problems
- Participate in prospective client pitches and be the best representative for the Aladdin Wealth platform

Skills

- Strong fundamental understanding of technology and financial analytics
- Outstanding communication and presentation skills, with the ability to convey sophisticated concepts simply and clearly to clients
- The ability to face-off to external clients with solid project management and coordination skills, balancing multiple priorities simultaneously
- The desire to have a deep understanding of analytics and our clients, applying technology to solve real business needs
- The ability to thrive under pressure and enjoy a fast-paced environment, as well as set and meet tight deadlines
- Experience in software engineering/development (e.g., Python, Java, R, SQL, Unix)
- Experience implementing scalable processes, as a plus
- Experience within the Wealth Management industry, as a plus

### Benefits

- Annual discretionary bonus
- Healthcare
- Leave benefits
- Retirement benefits
- Strong retirement plan
- Tuition reimbursement
- Comprehensive healthcare
- Support for working parents
- Flexible Time Off (FTO)

Company Overview

- BlackRock is an investment company that offers its services to institutions, intermediaries, foundations, and individual investors. It was founded in 1988, and is headquartered in New York, New York, USA, with a workforce of 10001+ employees. Its website is http://www.blackrock.com. Company H1B Sponsorship

results.title: Senior Quantitative Developer, Director | Wfh
highlights:
Senior Quantitative Developer, Director | Wfh

Wfh.← All Jobs

# Senior Quantitative Developer, Director

Posted Jun 3, 2026

### About the position

About this role Quantitative Modeling and Research (QMR) is a core team within the Single Security Modeling group at BlackRock. The team is responsible for designing, building, and maintaining sophisticated risk and valuation models that support a broad range of asset classes, including interest rates, foreign exchange, inflation, equities, and credit. QMR plays a critical role in powering BlackRock’s investment platform and Aladdin. We are currently undertaking a multi‑year modernization of our core analytics libraries and services and are seeking a senior quantitative developer to help lead this effort. Role Overview This role is a senior technical position within QMR, focused on the design and evolution of large‑scale quantitative analytics libraries. The successful candidate will contribute to shaping the technical direction, engineering standards, and long‑term sustainability of the analytics platform that underpins Aladdin. The role combines deep quantitative engineering, modern C++ development, and close alignment with real‑world business requirements.

- Responsibilities
- Play a senior technical role in a multi‑year initiative to modernize BlackRock’s core analytics infrastructure, influencing architecture, engineering standards, and best practices.
- Design, build, and maintain large‑scale C++ analytics libraries with a strong emphasis on performance, correctness, extensibility, and long‑term maintainability in a production environment operating at massive scale.
- Act as a technical leader on complex initiatives by driving design discussions, reviewing architecture and code, mentoring other quantitative developers, and raising overall code quality.
- Lead the design of modern frameworks and abstractions for quantitative models, maintaining a pragmatic engineering mindset grounded in real‑world business needs.
- Champion modern C++ practices, tooling, and workflows, including testing strategies, performance profiling, CI/CD integration, and responsible use of emerging AI‑assisted development tools.
- Leverage modern hardware and parallel computing techniques to deliver analytics efficiently at scale while maintaining clarity and discipline in the codebase.
- Collaborate closely with quantitative researchers, engineers, and business partners across Aladdin.

- Requirements
- 7+ years of industry experience in quantitative development or quantitative engineering.
- Deep experience designing and building quantitative libraries in C++.
- Strong experience implementing complex mathematical and financial models.
- Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Engineering, or a related quantitative discipline.
- Deep familiarity with industry best practices for software engineering, including code quality, testing, and maintainability.
- Strong communication skills and the ability to operate effectively in a collaborative, global environment.

- Nice-to-haves
- Experience modernizing large, legacy analytics or modeling codebases.
- Exposure to AI‑assisted development tools and an understanding of the modern AI coding landscape.
- Experience with performance‑critical systems and parallel or high‑performance computing.

- Benefits
- employees are eligible for an annual discretionary bonus, and benefits including healthcare, leave benefits, and retirement benefits
- strong retirement plan
- tuition reimbursement
- comprehensive healthcare
- support for working parents
- Flexible Time Off (FTO)

Apply for This Job

BlackRock is hiring candidates for the role of Analyst, Data Management for the Bengaluru, Karnataka, India locations. The complete details about BlackRock Recruitment Program 2026 are as follows.

| Job ID | R264612 |

Learn DataAnalytics,Data Science & AI From Top Data Experts - Unlock Opportunities with 500+ Elite Hiring Partners 😍

- Job Position: Analyst, Data Management
- Job Location: Bengaluru, Karnataka, India
- Salary Package: As per Company Standards
- Full/Part Time: Full Time
- Req ID: 264612
- Education Level: Graduation

#### Qualifications

What you’ll bring to us:

- You have a Bachelors’ Degree or equivalent experience in Finance, Accounting, Business or Engineering
- You are proficient in English and have excellent writing and interpersonal skills
- Minimum 1+ years of proven work experience.
- High level understanding of private markets or private equity.
- You have a keen curiosity about finance and knowledge of financial markets
- You have experience using Microsoft tools such as SQL, Copilot, Excel, SharePoint, and Office 365.
- Good to have python skills.
- Learn new tools, workflows and datasets.

What you’ll be doing:

- Acquire, update and maintain high-quality alternatives data using various internal tools and software
- Conduct in-depth web-based research to collect data relating to firms such as Company Information, Deals, Investments, Valuation and other key data points of the firm.
- Interpret company filings, deal information, and structured/unstructured data.
- Update Preqin’s databases with key financial information, statistics, and analysis on which our clients depend on
- Work on complex data projects related to data quality, process re-engineering and workflow optimization
- Support bulk updates, backfills, and data improvement initiatives as required
- Maintain clear documentation and audit trails for research decisions
- Develop subject matter expertise to improve our data offering to clients.
- Work with collaborators across various business units to further develop our data product offering

#### Skills Required

This role sits within Preqin, a part of BlackRock. Preqin plays a key role in how we are revolutionizing private markets data and technology for clients globally, complementing our existing Aladdin technology platform to deliver investment solutions for the whole portfolio.

The Data team is responsible for ensuring that Preqin’s coverage of the alternative assets industry is accurate, up to date and comprehensive. Your primary focus will be on data processing and quality assurance of public data. You will report into the Data Lead, and work in close collaboration with a global team of data management specialists to research, analyze, and curate public data delivered to Preqin’s clients.

highlights:
# BlackRock Hiring MS Dynamics CRM Developer 2026: Associate Role in Gurgaon
[...]
BlackRock, the world’s largest asset manager with over $10 trillion in AUM, is recruiting for an MS Dynamics CRM Developer/Engineer, Associate at its Gurgaon office. This role is part of the Dynamics Solution Center, which supports BlackRock’s Sales and Service teams globally. As the company continues to digitize client interactions and streamline internal workflows, proficiency in Microsoft Dynamics 365 and Power Platform is critical. This position offers a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of finance and enterprise CRM technology within a top-tier fintech environment.
[...]
| Company | BlackRock |

| Location | Gurgaon, India |

| Salary | As per Company Standards (Estimated ₹12-18 LPA) |

The role requires a minimum of 4+ years in a technical role, with at least 1 year dedicated to Dynamics 365 and/or Power Platform engineering. Candidates with a Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field (e.g., Computer Science, Information Systems) are preferred. A solid grasp of D365 Sales and Customer Service applications is mandatory. While experience with JavaScript, C#, or .NET is listed as a benefit, it is increasingly becoming a differentiator in technical assessments. The role demands excellent communication skills to explain technical concepts to non-technical senior stakeholders, so candidates with prior client-facing or cross-functional collaboration experience will have an edge.

Generative artificial intelligence is transforming how we build products, serve clients, and operate at scale. In the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help turn advanced models into dependable, secure, and high-performing production services. You will work with partners across machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering to deliver solutions with clear return on investment. If you enjoy hands-on engineering, real-world constraints, and high-impact delivery, this role is for you. As a Senior Associate, Generative AI Engineer in the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help design, build, and support production generative artificial intelligence products and reusable backend application programming interfaces used across the firm. You will combine large enterprise datasets with large language and multimodal models to deliver scalable, measurable solutions. You will collaborate closely with machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering partners to ensure reliability, performance, and strong operational controls. results.title: Senior Deep Learning Scientist, Risk Labs | Hirro
highlights:
Scientist, Risk Labs

Remote, USA

Posted 2026-05-28

Full-time

Remote Posted Date : 07 / 03 / 25 Posted 3 days ago Block is one company built from many blocks, all united by the same purpose of economic empowerment. The blocks that form our foundational teams — People, Finance, Counsel, Hardware, Information Security, Platform Infrastructure Engineering, and more — provide support and guidance at the corporate level. They work across business groups and around the globe, spanning time zones and disciplines to develop inclusive People policies, forecast finances, give legal counsel, safeguard systems, nurture new initiatives, and more. Every challenge creates possibilities, and we need different perspectives to see them all. Bring yours to Block. The Role Since Block's inception, our innovative and technology-forward approach to risk management and customer protection has been fundamental to how we invent and build financial products. The Risk team at Block continues this legacy through a sophisticated, technology and science-led approach to protecting our customers and their funds. Our interdisciplinary structure combines Product Development, Science teams (specializing in modeling, analytics, and data science), Operations and key partners including Legal, Compliance and Policy, all working in concert to identify, assess, and solve complex risk challenges across access, fraud prevention and compliance. This role is part of our Risk Labs team - this team's mission is aimed at developing advanced deep learning and reinforcement learning based signals and learned representations for machine learning models. In this role, you'll explore, develop and implement alternatives to traditional feature based ML with state-of-the-art models for solving risk problems. This team works across a wide-swath of the Risk organization touching a variety of use cases and collaborating with a diverse set of teams. What You'll Be Up To Reporting to the lead of the Risk Labs Modeling team Dreaming up, designing, and running big experiments to quickly test out cool (and practical) ideas Applying the latest theoretical breakthroughs to make our products even better Building experiments, prototypes, and architectural designs across computer science fields like ML, data mining, NLP, and performance analysis Contributing to the growth of new initiatives by sharing emerging trends and best practices, fostering a collaborative and innovative environment Taking the lead on defining data structures, frameworks, designs, and evaluation metrics for your research solutions – you'll have a lot of autonomy here Teaming up with folks inside and outside Block to find new research areas and shape our long-term strategies Running experiments for your research, using simulations and prototypes to see what happens Refining how we do research, solidifying your hypotheses, and helping evolve our practices with minimal hand-holding A Level 6 hire, which usually means something like : Someone who's been through the whole ML cycle, with a big plus for experience on the MLE / deployment side Proven experience in conducting applied research projects with tangible outcomes Some intellectual property under your belt - these contributions could take the form of open source software, patents, or refereed publications in venues such as conferences, journals, books, etc. Experience with AI, deep learning, or reinforcement learning is key, and bonus points if you've done unique things with LLMs Excellent problem-solving abilities and a creative mindset to tackle complex challenges A great communicator - someone who can easily chat with different cross-functional teams and contribute to the research community Technologies We Use and Teach MySQL, Snowflake, GCP / AWS and Spark We're working to build a more inclusive economy where our customers have equal access to opportunity, and we strive to live by these same values in building our workplace. Block is an equal opportunity

results.title: Director, Cloud Automation Engineer | Hirevector
highlights: the speed of a startup, but want to solve some of the world’s most complex problems? Do you want to work with, and learn from, hands-on leaders in technology and finance? At BlackRock, we are looking for Engineers who like to innovate and solve complex problems. We recognize that strength comes from diversity, and will embrace your unique skills, curiosity, drive, and passion while giving you the opportunity to grow technically and as an individual. With over USD $8 trillion of assets, we have an exceptional responsibility: our technology empowers millions of investors to save for retirement, pay for college, buy a home, and improve their financial wellbeing. Being a developer at BlackRock means you get the best of both worlds: working for one of the most advanced financial companies and being part of an engineering team responsible for next generation technology and solutions. Team Overview This position requires a unique blend of hands-on systems knowledge, software development skills and team leadership. The global System Operations Platform team is a sizable team of over 50 individuals located in six different regions that perform engineering and operational work in support of critical infrastructure hosted on prem & on cloud. Our team is on the front lines migrating the Aladdin Platform to the cloud and containers. We are in search of a Principal Engineer to help lead Compute engineering. This role requires a visionary leader and hands-on builder who is also able to collaborate globally to drive architecture and engineering excellence across the breadth of our organization. We seek a multi-disciplinary expert with a strong desire to mentor and effect organizational change. We require this unique combination as we enhance the platform products and services that we host across multiple clouds for the larger Aladdin engineering community. You are: Curious and eager to learn new things, with a healthy disrespect for the status quo. A big thinker, willing to tackle projects that require both technical and organizational creativity to be successful. Passionate about technology, with personal ownership for the work you do. Data-focused, with an eye for the details that matter to solve the problem. A leader who invests as much time in architecting engineering practices as they do in the technology itself We are: Passionate about building quality teams and scaling our technology to meet the needs of tomorrow. Building solutions for BlackRock and over 100 organizations who use our technology. Developing in Java, Go, Python with a strong bias towards Containers, Cloud-Native distributed containerized microservice orchestration., and emerging IaC technologies. Product-oriented with a drive towards continuous integration and deployment with a focus on incremental innovation. Requirements • Minimum of 10 – 15 years software or systems engineering work experience on large scale systems development and deployment. • BA/BS in Computer Science (or similar) from an accredited university (or in lieu of degree, 4+ years of relevant work experience). • Experience in a Linux environment with Kubernetes/containers experience • Experience with cloud deployment technology tools (Open Container Initiative (OCI) container packaging and runtime, Automation tools for configuration management and deployment, Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools for provisioning cloud resources., Cross plane, etc.) • Familiarity with modern DevOps / Nexora/CD, Observability and Agile SDLC is required. • Team lead skills with experience leading global teams, defining strategic architectures, implementing process transformation and driving projects to completion. • Strong programming skills in Python, Go, Java , or similar. • Expert experience with modern Infrastructure as Code platforms such as Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools for provisioning cloud resources, Crossplane or Pulumi • Proficient leading Agile development processes • Architectural

all mandates conform to performance expectations, investment guidelines, risk parameters, and regulatory requirements. • Utilize technology and analytical tools to improve processes and drive scale. • Build processes to solve investment challenges and integrate them with existing BlackRock infrastructure (i.e. Aladdin). • Coordinate with analytics, risk, data and other platform teams to drive improvements in systems, investment and trading processes. • Explore and apply AI and machine learning techniques, including prompt engineering, to advance scalable analytics and streamline processes. • Collaborate with other portfolio managers to translate investment workflows into scalable technology solutions to mitigate risk and improve efficiency. # Database Hosting-Analyst
[...]
BlackRock is a global leader in investment management and financial technology. The Database Hosting Analyst will engage in global operations and engineering strategy for mission-critical database platforms, ensuring reliability and performance of financial systems while collaborating on innovation and automation. Responsibilities Deep engagement in administration and engineering of OLTP, OLAP, data warehousing, and distributed NoSQL platforms Ensure 24x7 availability and resiliency for global investment systems Scale operations through advanced automation frameworks and custom toolsets Leverage LLM tools including Windsurf, Copilot to enhance monitoring and maintenance utilities in Python, Perl and Java Drive adoption of DevOps practices for database deployments and monitoring Engage with infrastructure, application, and business teams across regions to deliver seamless service Influence technology roadmaps and participate in architecture reviews Skills Bachelor's in computer science or related field 1+ years in enterprise database administration & engineering 1+ years in software engineering, LLM/AI Deep engagement in administration and engineering of OLTP, OLAP, data warehousing, and distributed NoSQL platforms Ensure 24x7 availability and resiliency for global investment systems Scale operations through advanced automation frameworks and custom toolsets Leverage LLM tools including Windsurf, Copilot to enhance monitoring and maintenance utilities in Python, Perl and Java Drive adoption of DevOps practices for database deployments and monitoring Engage with infrastructure, application, and business teams across regions to deliver seamless service Influence technology roadmaps and participate in architecture reviews Strong learning agility and adaptability Self-starter with a drive for superior performance Natural curiosity and commitment to innovation Platforms: Sybase ASE/IQ, Microsoft SQL Server, Snowflake, Cassandra, Cosmos DB Replication: Sybase Replication, MSSQL HA, HVR/Fivetran Cloud: Azure, AWS, GCP Automation & DevOps: Python, Perl, Java, Go, Windsurf, Copilot, Ansible/AWX, Git, Azure DevOps OS: Linux, Windows; advanced shell scripting and system performance analysis Benefits Annual discretionary bonus Healthcare Leave benefits Retirement benefits Strong retirement plan Tuition reimbursement Comprehensive healthcare Support for working parents Flexible Time Off (FTO) Company Overview BlackRock is an investment company that offers its services to institutions, intermediaries, foundations, and individual investors. It was founded in 1988, and is headquartered in New York, New York, USA, with a workforce of 10001+ employees. Its website is http://www.blackrock.com. Company H1B Sponsorship BlackRock has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 1 in 2026, 18 in 2025, 34 in 2024, 7 in 2023, 37 in 2022, 31 in 2021, 27 in 2020. results.title: Senior Machine Learning Scientist – NLP People
highlights:
Senior Machine Learning Scientist – NLP People
[...]
Senior Data Scientist
[...]
🦸‍♀️ The Role 🦸‍♀️
[...]
Our mission is to help large successful brands like Uber, Amazon, Wise, HelloFresh (and more!) put their customers at the centre of everything they do. Using best-in-class tech in a fast-developing AI space, our Customer Experience Intelligence platform continuously analyses explicit and implicit feedback to enable our clients to identify what they should do next.
[...]
We’re hiring a Senior Data Scientist to join the team and help build and ship the next generation of that stack.
[...]
Unlike many companies, we use our own custom models, specialised for customer feedback, across various parts of the stack: extraction, retrieval, reranking, summarisation, and sentiment analysis. We are also pragmatic and understand that the right solution can be a combination of off-the-shelf LLMs, bespoke fine-tuned models, and sometimes techniques that utilise no LLM at all.
[...]
Train, evaluate, and iterate on ML models and agentic systems for customer feedback, including owning our custom fine-tuning pipelines. Run experiments end-to-end, track results rigorously, and make clear recommendations on what to ship, iterate, or retire.
[...]
Build and maintain LLM-powered features: retrieval pipelines, reranking systems, insight agents, data mining agents, and automated taxonomy generation.
[...]
Design and run robust evaluation frameworks: build test sets, define metrics, evaluate non-deterministic systems, handle class imbalance, and automate checkpoint comparisons.
[...]
Improve and extend semantic search and retrieval, evolving from embedding-based approaches toward more advanced methods.
[...]
Write production-quality code and collaborate closely with Engineering on productionisation, model serving, data pipelines, and monitoring.
[...]
Work with Product and Commercial teams to translate business needs into practical ML solutions, and support client evaluations and accuracy benchmarking.
[...]
🧰 What you’ll need:
[...]
A deep working knowledge of transformer architectures.
[...]
Strong PyTorch skills, with the ability to write custom training loops, modify model architectures, and debug issues at the tensor level. Ideally, experience with parameter-efficient fine-tuning techniques such as LoRA
[...]
Extensive experience working with large-scale, messy real-world text data, including classification, extraction, embeddings, re-rankers, clustering, and search.
[...]
Experience in instruction fine-tuning and serving language models, familiarity with frameworks such as vLLM, DeepSpeed, or similar tools
[...]
A solid grounding in classical ML and statistics, and the judgement to choose simpler methods when they’re the right solution.
[...]
Practical experience building with GenAI and agentic patterns.
[...]
Technical curiosity and a keen interest in AI – a love of experimenting to make the most of available technology.
[...]
MSc/PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Computational Linguistics or a closely related STEM field.
[...]
Experience with reinforcement learning techniques, such as with verifiable reward (RLVR)
[...]
#machinelearning #machinelearningscientist #seniormachinelearningscientist #datascience #deeplearning #LLM #ML #data #AI #PyTorch
[...]
